title: Rich Meaty Russian Borscht Stew
description: A hearty, malty Russian stew with a rich flavor profile featuring cardamom notes and low-calorie-friendly ingredients. This adventurous dish is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the beef stew meat and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the browned beef from the pot and set aside. Add the chopped onions to the pot and cook until they're translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the sliced carrots and cook for an additional 5 minutes. The carrots should be tender but still crisp.
Add the sliced beets, minced garlic, crushed cardamom, and ground allspice to the pot. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the tomato paste to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes. The paste should be fragrant and starting to darken in color.
Add the browned beef back to the pot, along with the beef broth and red wine vinegar. Stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2-3 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly. Strain the stew through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Return the pot to the stovetop and bring the stew to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has thickened slightly.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Deglaze the pot by adding 1 tablespoon of flour and 1 cup of water. Stir to combine and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Strain the deglazing liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into the stew, discarding the solids.
